description: Performance audit (2026-03-04) confirmed portfolio VM still builds request-data (including daily-compounded-returns) before signature gating in /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/views/portfolio/vm.cljs (lines ~1131-1143 and ~1197-1205). Reorder pipeline so signature is computed first and short-circuits request-data + daily row derivation when unchanged. Capture before/after timings for repeated renders with unchanged source versions.
title: Compute portfolio metrics request signature before heavy row derivation

description: In Ghost Mode modal, watchlist list items render as a literal hiccup/closure vector string instead of li rows. Fix list child composition and add regression coverage.
notes: Root cause: ghost mode watchlist modal rendered children as [:ul ... (mapv ...)] which produced a nested vector child interpreted as text in UI renderer. Fix: use (into [:ul ...] (map ...)) so each watchlist row is emitted as a proper sibling child. Added regression assertion in test/hyperopen/views/app_shell_spacing_test.cljs to require ghost-mode-watchlist-row presence and reject literal "[[:li" string leakage.
title: Ghost mode watchlist renders hiccup vector literal

acceptance_criteria: 1) /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs becomes a thin facade with no cross-domain business logic. 2) Subdomain namespaces exist for websocket, asset-selector, wallet, order, funding, and vault adapters with clear ownership. 3) Existing runtime wiring continues to use stable public vars without behavior regression. 4) Required gates pass: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket.
description: Refactor /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into bounded subdomain adapter namespaces so runtime seams are easier to reason about and safer for future agent edits. Current evidence: file is 820 LOC (821 with trailing newline) and has 40 require aliases, the highest fan-out in src/hyperopen. It currently mixes websocket, asset-selector, wallet, order, funding, and vault workflows in one module. Keep the public namespace hyperopen.runtime.effect-adapters stable during migration while implementation moves behind subdomain modules.
title: Decompose runtime/effect_adapters by subdomain with stable facade

acceptance_criteria: 1) New subdirectory /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters/ exists with a documented module map. 2) Facade namespace still resolves all existing public symbols and call signatures. 3) No behavior change in runtime wiring. 4) Targeted tests for facade export parity are present.
description: Introduce /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters/ namespace tree and move shared helpers (effect-handler wrappers, storage/navigation adapters, shared error helpers) to a small common module. Keep /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs as the stable facade namespace that re-exports all existing public vars used by /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/app/effects.cljs and /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/core/compat.cljs.
title: Create effect-adapters subdomain scaffold and facade export map

acceptance_criteria: 1) Websocket + diagnostics adapter functions are owned by websocket subdomain namespace(s). 2) Behavior and signatures remain identical at facade exports. 3) Existing websocket/runtime tests continue to pass and include at least one regression asserting health diagnostics enrichment parity.
description: Move websocket runtime adapter implementations out of /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into dedicated websocket-focused namespace(s), including init/reconnect/refresh health, active-asset/orderbook/trades/webdata2 subscribe-unsubscribe adapters, diagnostics copy/reset/reveal handlers, and restore-active-asset startup seam. Preserve deterministic effect ordering and health diagnostics payload shape.
title: Extract websocket and diagnostics effect adapters into websocket subdomain module

acceptance_criteria: 1) Asset-selector adapter logic lives outside the monolith file in dedicated namespace(s). 2) Runtime behavior remains stable for cache restore/persist and context subscription diffing. 3) Tests cover queue flush scheduling and owner-scoped active-ctx subscription parity.
description: Move asset-selector-specific runtime adapters out of /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into an asset-selector subdomain module. Scope includes icon status queue/flush scheduling, market cache persist/restore, active market display persist/load, and selector-visible active-asset-ctx subscription diffing logic.
title: Extract asset-selector effect adapters into dedicated subdomain module

acceptance_criteria: 1) Wallet adapter functions and timeout helpers are isolated in wallet namespace(s). 2) Facade exports remain backward compatible for app/effects wiring. 3) Wallet copy feedback and disconnect cleanup tests remain green with parity assertions.
description: Move wallet runtime adapter logic into wallet-focused namespace(s), including connect/disconnect, storage mode mutation, copy-wallet-address feedback lifecycle, and timeout scheduling/cleanup seams that currently sit in runtime/effect_adapters.cljs. Preserve deterministic UI feedback behavior and runtime timeout ownership boundaries.
title: Extract wallet effect adapters into wallet subdomain module

acceptance_criteria: 1) Order adapter wrappers and toast helper pipeline live in dedicated order namespace(s). 2) make-api-* factories preserve signatures used by app/effects runtime wiring. 3) Regression tests verify no behavior change for toast scheduling and API error projection.
description: Move order submission/cancel/position TPSL/margin adapter wrappers and shared order feedback toast helpers out of runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into an order subdomain module. Keep injected collaborator seams explicit (dispatch, runtime error normalization, prune canceled open orders) and preserve toast timing behavior.
title: Extract order effect adapters into order subdomain module

acceptance_criteria: 1) Funding adapter implementations are isolated from unrelated domains. 2) Funding predictability loading/success/error behavior stays unchanged. 3) Funding workflow submit/fetch effects remain wired and covered by regression tests.
description: Move funding-related adapter logic from runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into funding namespace(s): active-asset funding predictability state projection flow plus funding workflow API effects (fee estimate, withdrawal queue, transfer, withdraw, deposit). Maintain deterministic loading/error projection semantics and runtime-error normalization.
title: Extract funding effect adapters into funding subdomain module

acceptance_criteria: 1) Vault adapter wrappers are isolated in vaults namespace(s). 2) Existing app/effects API map keys continue to resolve through stable facade exports. 3) Vault API effect tests and wiring tests pass without behavior regressions.
description: Move vault-oriented runtime API adapter wrappers from runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into a vaults-focused namespace, including index/summaries/user-equities/detail/webdata2/fills/funding-history/order-history/ledger-updates fetch effects and vault transfer submit effect. Preserve injected collaborator seams and toast/error normalization contracts.
title: Extract vault effect adapters into vaults subdomain module

acceptance_criteria: 1) Runtime effect adapter tests are organized by subdomain (websocket, asset-selector, wallet, order, funding, vaults, facade). 2) Facade export contract test protects against accidental public API drift. 3) Full required gates pass: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket.
description: Refactor /hyperopen/test/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters_test.cljs into subdomain-oriented test namespaces that mirror the new runtime/effect_adapters/* module boundaries. Add explicit facade parity tests that ensure /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs continues exporting the required stable public API used by app/effects and core/compat macros.
title: Split effect adapter tests by subdomain and add facade parity contract coverage

description: Refactor /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/funding/actions.cljs and /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/funding/effects.cljs into explicit domain, application, and infrastructure boundaries while preserving runtime action/effect contracts and funding modal behavior.
notes:
Implemented first refactor slice: funding.actions is now a facade over funding.application.modal-actions; extracted lifecycle normalization to funding.domain.lifecycle; extracted HyperUnit request/fallback client to funding.infrastructure.hyperunit-client; funding.effects now consumes domain/infrastructure seams. Required gates currently pass (npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket). Remaining: split modal view-model/action orchestration and effects submit/polling orchestration into smaller application modules.
2026-03-03: Completed route-client infrastructure extraction slice. Added src/hyperopen/funding/infrastructure/route_clients.cljs for LiFi/Across URL construction + payload parsing, rewired funding/effects.cljs via compatibility wrappers, and validated with npm run check, npm test, and npm run test:websocket (all green).
2026-03-03: Completed wallet-RPC infrastructure extraction slice. Added src/hyperopen/funding/infrastructure/wallet_rpc.cljs (provider request, chain switch, tx receipt polling, send+confirm helper), rewired funding/effects.cljs through private compatibility aliases, and re-ran npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket (all green).
2026-03-03: Completed submit-orchestration application split. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/submit_effects.cljs for transfer/withdraw/deposit submit flows and converted funding/effects.cljs entrypoints to compatibility wrappers with preserved defaults. Validation: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket all green.
2026-03-03: Completed lifecycle-polling application split. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/lifecycle_polling.cljs for polling orchestration and rewired funding/effects.cljs polling entrypoints to compatibility wrappers with injected seams. Validation: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket all green.
2026-03-03: Completed modal view-model application split. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/modal_vm.cljs and replaced modal_actions funding-modal-view-model with a compatibility wrapper that injects existing helper seams. Validation: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket all green.
2026-03-03: Completed modal command split + HyperUnit address client extraction. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/modal_commands.cljs and rewired modal_actions command entrypoints as compatibility wrappers. Added src/hyperopen/funding/infrastructure/hyperunit_address_client.cljs and rewired funding/effects.cljs HyperUnit address request/fallback helpers through compatibility wrappers. Validation: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket all green.
2026-03-03: Completed ERC20 RPC infrastructure extraction. Added src/hyperopen/funding/infrastructure/erc20_rpc.cljs for calldata encoding and eth_call balance/allowance reads; rewired funding/effects.cljs to use compatibility aliases/wrappers. Validation: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket all green.
2026-03-03: Completed domain-policy and deposit-transaction extraction slice. Added src/hyperopen/funding/domain/policy.cljs for modal parsing/preview/fee-display/lifecycle status policy and rewired src/hyperopen/funding/application/modal_actions.cljs as a thin compatibility/composition facade. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/deposit_submit.cljs for Bridge2/LiFi/Across deposit transaction orchestration and rewired src/hyperopen/funding/effects.cljs through dependency-injected wrappers. Validation: npx shadow-cljs compile app, npx shadow-cljs compile test, npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket all passed.
2026-03-03: Completed HyperUnit query orchestration extraction. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/hyperunit_query.cljs and rewired src/hyperopen/funding/effects.cljs wrappers for existing-address selection/prefetch plus fee/withdrawal-queue fetch flows, preserving private-var test seams. Validation rerun: npx shadow-cljs compile app, npx shadow-cljs compile test, npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket (all green).
2026-03-03: Committed refactor slices as 1d7d18f and updated ExecPlan. Remote landing remains blocked in this environment: git pull/push failed with GitHub SSH auth (publickey), and bd dolt push has no configured remote origin.
2026-03-03: Completed optional final lifecycle-helper cleanup slice. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/lifecycle_guards.cljs for modal activity + poll-token guard ownership and src/hyperopen/funding/domain/lifecycle_operations.cljs for operation selection, lifecycle conversion, and polling delay policy. Rewired src/hyperopen/funding/effects.cljs through compatibility wrappers/aliases and reduced file size to ~834 LOC. Validation rerun: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket (all green).
title: Funding bounded-context split (domain/application/infrastructure)

description: Performance audit (2026-03-04) found the position overlay repaint path rebuilding overlay DOM on every repaint in /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/views/trading_chart/utils/chart_interop/position_overlays.cljs (clear-children! + rebuild at lines ~758-813). Recent overlay chip additions increased per-repaint node/style work. Implement incremental overlay updates (reuse existing row/chip nodes and only patch coordinates/text/styles on change), then measure scripting time and node mutation count under websocket/timescale churn.
notes: Implemented in /Users/barry/.codex/worktrees/19fa/hyperopen: position_overlays now reuses cached PNL/liquidation DOM rows and patches styles/text/coordinates in place without root clear+rebuild per repaint. Added regression test position-overlays-reuses-dom-rows-across-overlay-updates-test to assert node identity reuse and content updates. Validation passed: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket.
title: Reduce chart position overlay repaint DOM churn

description: In Ghost Mode modal, pasted addresses are not accepted by Add to Watchlist or Start Spectating. Investigate modal input wiring and action payload path so pasted addresses are persisted and spectating can start.
notes:
Root cause: schema contract  incorrectly required exactly one arg, but Ghost Mode modal dispatches  and  with zero args (using current search state). In debug/runtime validation this blocked both actions. Fix: allow zero-or-one value in , and add contract tests for zero/one-arg ghost-mode actions and portfolio benchmark action parity.
Correction: Root cause is the schema contract optional-string-args required exactly one arg, while Ghost Mode modal dispatches actions/start-ghost-mode and actions/add-ghost-mode-watchlist-address with zero args (using current search state). Runtime validation blocked both actions. Fix: optional-string-args now allows zero-or-one value; added contracts tests in test/hyperopen/schema/contracts_test.cljs for zero/one-arg ghost-mode actions and zero-arg select-portfolio-returns-benchmark parity.
title: Ghost Mode modal paste/start spectate broken

description: Implement the new order-filled toast UX to match the provided visual direction and replace count-based copy with execution-batch token totals and average price messaging. Scope includes websocket fill aggregation logic, toast payload/runtime wiring, UI layout/styling, dismiss affordance, and regression coverage for both behavior and rendering.
title: Order-fill toast redesign with execution-batch summaries

description: Update the user fill websocket toast-content pipeline to summarize each execution batch by side and token instead of order count. Compute total filled size and weighted average price from batch rows, and handle missing/invalid numeric rows with deterministic fallbacks.
title: Compute execution-batch token totals and weighted average fill price

description: Introduce a dedicated action path to clear the active global toast so the new close icon can dismiss immediately. Wire the action through public actions, runtime registration catalog, and action-arg contracts without breaking existing auto-timeout behavior.
title: Add explicit dismiss action for global order-feedback toast

description: Add/adjust tests to cover execution-batch toast summarization math, coin/side labeling, structured toast rendering, and dismiss interaction. Ensure old count-based copy is removed and required validation commands remain green.
title: Expand websocket and view regression coverage for fill toasts

description: Refactor global toast rendering for order fills into a two-line layout with headline + average-price subline, success icon treatment, and right-aligned dismiss affordance that matches the supplied screenshots while preserving accessibility and responsive behavior.
title: Redesign order-fill toast layout to match provided visual style

description: Refactor toast runtime state from single active toast to a bounded stack/list so multiple incoming notifications can be shown concurrently. Define deterministic insertion order, independent auto-dismiss timing, and manual dismiss behavior per toast without regressing existing callers.
title: Support stacked global toasts for concurrent notifications

description: Replace Ghost Mode fullscreen overlay modal with an anchored floating panel near the Ghost Mode trigger. Remove blackout backdrop behavior and keep click-away close semantics using the same positioned overlay pattern used by funding/TP-SL surfaces.
title: Ghost mode uses anchored panel instead of fullscreen modal

description:
Observed parity gap: Hyperopen treats `dexAbstraction` accounts as unified, which causes standard accounts to render unified-style account value and balance composition.

Reference addresses validated on 2026-03-04 via trade.xyz ghost mode:
- Unified: `0x4096d3377ae5ade578daae8188804740c8b1da3e`
- Standard (DEX abstraction): `0x2ba553d9f990a3b66b03b2dc0d030dfc1c061036`

Evidence summary:
- trade.xyz unified address shows `Unified Account Value` and unified-style balance treatment.
- trade.xyz standard address shows `Account Value` and split `Spot` + `Perps` equity treatment.
- Hyperliquid `userAbstraction` returns:
  - unified address => `"unifiedAccount"`
  - standard address => `"dexAbstraction"`
- Hyperopen currently normalizes `"dexAbstraction"` to `:unified` in `/hyperopen/src/hyperopen/api/endpoints/account.cljs`.

Goal:
Implement account-mode detection and rendering parity so:
1. Unified accounts render unified account value semantics.
2. Standard accounts render standard account value semantics.
3. Ghost mode and owner-mode both use the same deterministic mode-resolution path.

Acceptance criteria:
- Account mode projection distinguishes at least `:unified` and `:standard` (or equivalent semantic split) for Hyperliquid abstraction payloads.
- Portfolio summary, account-equity panel, and balances projection respect the mode split.
- Regression tests cover both provided addresses as fixture snapshots for mode-sensitive rendering outputs.
- Required gates pass: `npm run check`, `npm test`, `npm run test:websocket`.
title: Account mode parity: unified vs standard account value rendering

description: Introduce explicit mapping for Hyperliquid abstraction modes so dexAbstraction is not collapsed into unified semantics; project mode deterministically for owner and ghost effective addresses.
title: Define canonical account-mode model for abstraction payloads

description: Update account-value labels and equity composition logic to match unified vs standard behavior across account-equity panel, portfolio summary, and balances rows.
title: Apply mode-aware rendering in equity/portfolio/balances views

description: Add deterministic tests for abstraction normalization and mode-sensitive VM/projection outputs, including coverage for the two parity addresses as fixtures.
title: Add regression tests for unified vs standard account-value behavior

description: Execute required quality gates and a manual ghost-mode parity check matrix against trade.xyz behavior; capture residual risks and follow-up items.
title: Run parity validation and capture rollout notes

description: Switching Ghost Mode spectated address could briefly apply websocket/HTTP payloads from the previously spectated address, causing balance/position/account-value flicker. Fix adds effective-address guards for user/webData2 websocket handlers and startup account bootstrap callbacks; stale payloads are ignored.
title: Ghost mode spectate switch applies stale address payloads

description:
Goal
Reduce rate-limit pressure by replacing repeated /info POST refresh fanout with WebSocket subscription-driven state updates where supported.

Scope
- Inventory all /info POST request payloads and classify by churn.
- Map each payload type to available Hyperliquid WS subscriptions or identify no-WS fallback.
- Implement phased WS-first migration with feature flags, observability, and rollback safety.

Definition of done
- High-frequency account/order refresh paths are WS-first.
- Unsupported endpoints are throttled/cached with explicit TTLs and dedupe.
- Metrics show reduced /info request volume and fewer rate-limit events.
title: Migrate high-churn Hyperliquid /info polling to WebSocket subscriptions

description:
Implement per-request-type telemetry for Hyperliquid /info calls and produce a baseline report.

Deliverables
- Counter/timer metrics tagged by request `type` and calling path.
- Baseline dashboard or report for request rate, burst patterns, and rate-limit responses.
- Identification of top 5 high-churn request paths to target first.
notes:
Implemented telemetry foundation in /src/hyperopen/api/info_client.cljs:
- Added per-type/per-source started/completed counters
- Added per-type/per-source latency aggregates (count/total/max ms)
- Added per-type/per-source rate-limit attribution
- Preserved legacy stats keys for compatibility
- Added fallback source attribution from dedupe-key before unknown

Tests:
- Added deterministic coverage in /test/hyperopen/api_test.cljs for new counters and attribution behavior
- Verified via `npx shadow-cljs compile ws-test && node out/ws-test.js` (298 tests, 1718 assertions, 0 failures)

Remaining:
- Produce baseline hotspot summary from live run telemetry
- Resolve environment blockers for full required gates (`npm run check`, `npm test`, `npm run test:websocket`)
title: Instrument and baseline /info POST volume by request type

description:
Replace immediate /info refresh bursts triggered by order submit/cancel and fill events with WebSocket-driven updates.

Scope
- Prefer `openOrders` subscriptions (per dex context) over repeated `frontendOpenOrders` posts.
- Prefer `clearinghouseState` subscription updates over repeated account snapshot posts where feasible.
- Keep bounded backstop fetches only for recovery/reconciliation paths.
notes:
Implemented first WS-first gating slice:
- Added stream selector helper in /src/hyperopen/websocket/health_projection.cljs (`find-live-topic-stream`, `topic-stream-live?`)
- Order mutation refresh path now skips `request-frontend-open-orders!` when `openOrders` stream is live for user
- Order mutation refresh path now skips default `request-clearinghouse-state!` when `webData2` stream is live for user
- User fill/ledger refresh path now applies same skip behavior
- Spot and per-dex clearinghouse backstop refreshes retained

Tests added/updated:
- /test/hyperopen/websocket/health_projection_test.cljs
- /test/hyperopen/core_bootstrap/order_effects_test.cljs
- /test/hyperopen/websocket/user_test.cljs

Validation:
- `npx shadow-cljs compile ws-test && node out/ws-test.js` passed (301 tests, 1727 assertions)
- `npm run check` still blocked by missing dependency `@noble/secp256k1` in this environment
title: Migrate post-order and post-fill refresh fanout to WS-first updates

description:
Shift chart/timeframe data updates from repeated `candleSnapshot` POST calls to the WS `candle` subscription where supported.

Deliverables
- Subscription lifecycle tied to active symbol/timeframe visibility.
- Backfill strategy for initial historical window and reconnect gaps.
- Validation that chart correctness is maintained across interval switches.
title: Replace candleSnapshot polling with candle subscriptions

description:
Refactor startup/runtime account initialization to avoid per-dex /info fanout spikes at launch.

Scope
- Subscribe first for live account/order streams.
- Use minimal one-shot REST snapshots only for required initial state and gap fill.
- Add deterministic ordering so user-visible state updates remain responsive before heavy fetch/subscription work.
notes:
Implemented WS-first startup bootstrap gating in this slice.

Code changes:
- /src/hyperopen/startup/runtime.cljs
  - Added startup stream live helper and delayed fallback scheduler for stream-covered startup surfaces
  - Stage A now delays fallback fetches for openOrders, userFills, and userFundings topics
  - Stage B now skips per-dex open-order refresh when openOrders stream is live
- /src/hyperopen/config.cljs
  - Added :startup :stream-backfill-delay-ms (450)
- /src/hyperopen/runtime/state.cljs
  - Exposed startup-stream-backfill-delay-ms
- /src/hyperopen/app/startup.cljs
  - Wired startup-stream-backfill-delay-ms into startup base deps

Tests:
- /test/hyperopen/startup/runtime_test.cljs
  - Added coverage for stage-B open-orders skip on live stream
  - Added coverage for startup stream-covered fetch skip when streams are live
  - Added coverage for delayed fallback execution when streams are not live
- /test/hyperopen/core_bootstrap/runtime_startup_test.cljs
  - Updated bootstrap integration fixture to run with zero startup stream backfill delay

Validation:
- Pass: npx shadow-cljs compile ws-test && node out/ws-test.js
  - Ran 304 tests containing 1741 assertions. 0 failures, 0 errors.
- Blocked: npm run check
  - Missing dependency @noble/secp256k1 during shadow-cljs compile app.
title: Convert account startup bootstrap to WS-first with bounded REST backfill

description:
For request types without direct WS parity (e.g., metadata, vault endpoints, fees/portfolio/funding history), reduce rate-limit pressure through smarter REST usage.

Deliverables
- Central TTL policy per endpoint type.
- In-flight dedupe/coalescing for identical concurrent requests.
- Route/visibility-based gating so inactive screens do not poll.
notes:
Implemented central non-subscribable /info hardening slice:
- Added /src/hyperopen/api/request_policy.cljs with endpoint TTL defaults
- Extended /src/hyperopen/api/info_client.cljs with TTL response cache keyed by cache-key/dedupe-key + force-refresh bypass
- Wired TTL+dedupe defaults in account/market/orders/vault endpoint modules
- Added route/tab gating to suppress inactive account-history/funding-comparison/vault fetch effects
- Updated regression tests across endpoints/effects/api
Validation:
- npx shadow-cljs compile ws-test && node out/ws-test.js => Ran 315 tests containing 1768 assertions. 0 failures, 0 errors.
Environment blockers:
- npm run test:websocket / npm test fail: shadow-cljs not on PATH
- npm run check fails at app compile: missing @noble/secp256k1
title: Harden non-subscribable /info endpoints with TTL, dedupe, and route gating

description:
Introduce feature flags to control WS-first behavior per flow and enable safe staged rollout.

Deliverables
- Flag controls for post-fill/order, startup bootstrap, and candle migration paths.
- Canary rollout plan with fallback toggles and runbook notes.
- Error budget guardrails to trigger automatic fallback when stream health degrades.
notes:
Implemented WS migration rollout controls with feature flags and health guardrails.

Code changes:
- Added /src/hyperopen/websocket/migration_flags.cljs
  - Config + state override resolution for ws-migration flags
  - Flow-specific guardrails for order/fill, startup bootstrap, and candle migration paths
  - Cache-aware candle REST backfill decision helper
- Updated /src/hyperopen/config.cljs

- Wired flags in flow seams:
  - /src/hyperopen/order/effects.cljs
  - /src/hyperopen/websocket/user.cljs
  - /src/hyperopen/startup/runtime.cljs
  - /src/hyperopen/chart/actions.cljs
  - /src/hyperopen/websocket/subscriptions_runtime.cljs

Docs:
- Added staged canary and rollback runbook: /docs/runbooks/ws-migration-rollout.md

Tests:

- Updated:
  - /test/hyperopen/core_bootstrap/chart_menu_and_storage_test.cljs
  - /test/hyperopen/websocket/subscriptions_runtime_test.cljs
  - /test/hyperopen/core_bootstrap/order_effects_test.cljs
  - /test/hyperopen/websocket/user_test.cljs
  - /test/hyperopen/startup/runtime_test.cljs

Validation:
- Pass: npx shadow-cljs compile ws-test && node out/ws-test.js
  - Ran 312 tests containing 1761 assertions. 0 failures, 0 errors.
- Pass: npm run lint:docs && npm run lint:docs:test
- Blocked: npm run check
  - Missing dependency @noble/secp256k1 during shadow-cljs compile app.
title: Roll out WS migration behind feature flags with staged canary

description:
Define and execute verification for success criteria after WS migration.

Acceptance
- /info POST volume reduced materially on key user journeys.
- Rate-limit response frequency reduced versus baseline.
- Regression tests updated/added for websocket and fallback behavior.
- Summary report attached to epic with before/after metrics.
title: Validate migration impact on rate limits and request load

description: Performance audit (2026-03-04) found O(n log n) sort work in render-triggered path: latest-entry-fill sorts matching fills each build in /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/views/trading_chart/utils/position_overlay_model.cljs (lines ~113-119), called from trading chart overlay model build in /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/views/trading_chart/core.cljs (lines ~398-409). Pre-index latest entry fill per asset/side (or memoize by stable fill source version) to remove repeated sort cost on chart updates.
title: Remove per-render fill sort from chart position overlay model

description: Implement Trade.xyz-style Ghost Mode in Hyperopen: spectate any address while preserving signer safety and deterministic runtime behavior. Scope includes account-context architecture seam, websocket/API address routing, UI entry/exit controls, read-only trading guardrails, watchlist+pinned addresses, and full regression coverage.
notes:
2026-03-04: Milestone 2 completed. Migrated read-side runtime/websocket/account-history/projection freshness routing to effective-account-address, added Ghost-mode effective-address regression coverage, and reran validation gates (npm test, npm run check, npm run test:websocket) all green.
2026-03-04: Milestone 3 completed. Added Ghost Mode action domain + runtime wiring, header entry controls, app-level active spectating banner with explicit stop control, modal/watchlist UI, and regression coverage in ghost_mode_actions/header_view/app_shell tests. Validation gates rerun and green (npm test, npm run check, npm run test:websocket).
2026-03-04: Milestone 4 completed. Added ghost-mode read-only mutation guardrails across trading/order/funding/vault action+effect seams, added order-ticket Stop Ghost Mode affordance and Cmd/Ctrl+X stop shortcut with editable-target collision guard, and published QA/runbook docs (/hyperopen/docs/qa/ghost-mode-manual-matrix.md, /hyperopen/docs/runbooks/ghost-mode-rollout.md). Validation gates rerun and green (npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket).
title: Ghost Mode account spectating

description: When Ghost Mode is active (read-only), hide Place Order and funding action buttons (Deposit, Perps <-> Spot, Withdraw) so informational components get more space and users see only relevant controls.
title: Ghost mode hides trade/funding action buttons

description: Performance audit (2026-03-04) found funding comparison VM rebuilding parse/filter/sort on each VM build in /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/views/funding_comparison/vm.cljs (lines ~251-257). Add memoization keyed by predicted-fundings identity/version + query + timeframe + sort + favorites signature so unchanged inputs skip full row pipeline. Include regression test or benchmark-style assertion for large market sets.
title: Add memoized cache for funding comparison parse/filter/sort pipeline
